commit.message:
Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

commit.message:
feat: Major SEO/GEO/AEO overhaul + 10 new high-demand tools

SEO Fixes:
- Remove fabricated AggregateRating from all schemas (Google penalty risk)
- Clean keyword stuffing from meta descriptions and OG titles
- Fix render-blocking CSS @imports (move Google Fonts to link tags)
- Remove auto-generated keyword-stuffed footer text
- Fix dateModified using static date instead of new Date()
- Remove wasteful OG image preloads
- Update copyright and keywords to 2026
- Remove duplicate schema definitions from json-ld.tsx
- Consolidate redirects to vercel.json (remove duplicates from next.config)
- Add 301 redirects for deleted landing pages (fix GSC 404 errors)

GEO/AEO (AI Search Optimization):
- Add llms.txt following llmstxt.org spec for AI discoverability
- Add llms-full.txt with comprehensive tool reference
- Add humans.txt for trust signals
- Update robots.txt to explicitly allow GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot
- Add caching headers for llms.txt files

New Tools (10):
- SVG to PNG, BMP to JPG, GIF to PNG, TIFF to PDF (Image)
- CSV to JSON, JSON to CSV, XML to JSON (Developer)
- Markdown to PDF, Base64 Encoder/Decoder, QR Code Generator (Utility)
- New "Developer & Data Tools" category in registry

SEO Infrastructure:
- Fix RelatedTools to show contextually relevant tools by category
- Add security headers (X-Frame-Options, Permissions-Policy)
- Create About page (E-E-A-T signal)
- Add 15 new programmatic SEO use cases for new tools

- Make brute-force params configurable (maxAuthAttempts, authLockoutMs)

commit.message:
Set contentHint='music' to force Opus music mode, revert to 128kbps MP3

The key fix: MediaStreamTrack.contentHint = 'music' tells Chromium's
WebRTC encoder to use Opus AUDIO mode instead of VOIP mode. VOIP mode
applies voice-optimized psychoacoustic processing that causes artifacts
on music (the "jittery/reverby" sound the user hears).

Also reverts SomaFM URLs to 128kbps MP3 (AAC URLs returned 404).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Phase 3 observability + VIP SLA column bug + alert source tracking

Critical bug: VIP SLA check (run_vip_sla_check) referenced whatsapp_messages.received_at
which doesn't exist — actual column is 'timestamp'. Query crashed every 5 min silently.
Same bug class as Session 11 Phase 3A fix.

Observability: All Phase 3 scheduled jobs now log completion even when there's nothing to
process. Previously, jobs returned silently when finding zero items, making it impossible
to distinguish "working correctly with no data" from "broken."

Alert source tracking: Added 'source' column to alerts table + create_alert() parameter.
All alert creation calls now pass source identifier (pipeline, calendar_prep, vip_sla,
deadline_cadence, commitment_check, rss_intelligence, calendar_protection, email_intelligence).

WhatsApp backfill: endpoint now runs in background (BackgroundTasks) instead of blocking
the HTTP response — 365-day backfill no longer times out.

Files: 10 modified (store_back, commitment_checker, deadline_manager, decision_engine,
pipeline, dashboard, init_database.sql, calendar_trigger, email_trigger, rss_trigger).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: baker-master
repository.description: Baker AI Chief of Staff - FastAPI backend + dashboard

commit.message:
fix: clean up leaked cluster-level roles in TestIgnorePrivileges (#346)

* feat: add support for trigger UPDATE OF columns (#342)

Triggers with column-specific UPDATE events (e.g., UPDATE OF email) were
losing the column specification during inspection, causing incorrect
migration plans that would fire triggers on all updates instead of only
on specified column changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: guard UPDATE OF column extraction with tgtype bitmask check

Only extract UPDATE OF columns when the trigger actually has an UPDATE
event, preventing false positives if the substring appears elsewhere.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: clean up leaked cluster-level roles in TestIgnorePrivileges

TestIgnorePrivileges creates cluster-level PostgreSQL roles (app_reader,
deploy_bot, admin_role) and ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES rules in the shared
embedded PG instance. Since Go runs tests alphabetically, these persist
and contaminate TestPlanAndApply, causing unexpected GRANT statements in
plan output for all table/view/index tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(sw): bumpar versión cache v3→v4 para purgar chunks Three.js obsoletos

El SW usaba stacklume-static-v3 en old y new SW por igual.
Al compartir el mismo nombre de cache, el nuevo SW heredaba el
antiguo f504d9358e54e939.js (con Environment HDR) y lo servía
indefinidamente con cache-first aunque el CDN ya tuviera código nuevo.
Al cambiar a v4, el activate del nuevo SW elimina v3 y descarga
recursos frescos, rompiendo el bucle de WebGL context loss en demo.

commit.message:
feat(px-alive): face tracking via Vilib camera

When Vilib detects a face, proportional pan/tilt servo control follows it.
Falls back to random gaze drift when no face detected for 3 seconds.
Camera runs in background thread via Vilib (picamera2 + Haar cascade).

- Configurable gains (FACE_TRACK_GAIN_PAN/TILT), deadzone, lost timeout
- --no-face flag to disable camera tracking
- Retry camera init (picamera2 can be stuck after unclean shutdown)
- Defers gaze drift and idle scan while actively tracking
- Graceful camera cleanup on shutdown

commit.message:
Fix override deny descriptions — it blocks traffic, not allows it

Override deny is the highest priority deny rule that blocks traffic even
when allow rules exist. Used for emergencies like isolating compromised
systems. Previous descriptions incorrectly said it "allows traffic."

commit.message:
Fix homepage: add SETUID/SETGID capabilities for su-exec

The entrypoint uses su-exec to drop from root to PUID/PGID, which
requires SETUID and SETGID capabilities and allowPrivilegeEscalation.
Without these, su-exec fails with 'Operation not permitted'.

commit.message:
refactor: unify OfficeLLM interface to QuickJS + serve (#409)

* refactor: unify OfficeLLM interface to QuickJS + serve

Route all office operations through QuickJS sandbox instead of direct
Tauri IPC, eliminating the three-interface confusion (office tool / bash
CLI / cove_interpreter). The office tool now accepts {command, args}
and generates JS that calls workspace.officellm() via run_js.

Changes:
- Rewrite office tool: {command, args} interface, executes via run_js
- Delete office-errors.ts (error handling in Rust/QuickJS layer)
- Auto-inject officellm_bridge.js into QuickJS when binary available
- Decouple diagram tool as built-in (no OfficeLLM skill dependency)
- Update system prompt and skill instructions for unified interface
- Update cove-core SKILL.md: document bridge API, simplify session rules
- Update office SKILL.md: focus on command reference, both entry points

Closes #408

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve save-as, status, save output, and diagram doc regressions

- workspace_officellm: validate path with may_not_exist for save command
  so save-as to a new file does not fail with NotFound
- formatOfficellmOutput: return "No active document session." when status
  data is null, instead of unhelpful "status: success"
- formatOfficellmOutput: wrap save path as "Document saved to: ..." so
  extract-file-paths.ts can produce file chips in the UI
- user-manual SKILL.md: move Diagram from skill-bundled to built-in
  (it uses beautiful-mermaid client-side, no OfficeLLM dependency)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce logo image download size by using 64px sizes prop

Lighthouse image-delivery audit showed logo.webp being served at 256px
but displayed at 65x48px CSS (114x84 device pixels at 1.75x DPR).

Changing sizes from "96px" to "64px" causes next/image to serve the
128px variant instead of the 256px variant on typical mobile devices,
saving approximately 8-12 KiB per page load.

fix(hub): corrigir recursão infinita no fechar do Em Breve

_fecharEmBreve() só faz cleanup (overlay + RAF + timer + styles).
Navegação de volta ao hub fica nos chamadores:
- tap/touchstart → fecharETornar() = _fecharEmBreve + fecharJoguinhos
- auto-close timer → idem
- voltarDoJogo → jogoAtual.fechar() → _fecharEmBreve (sem re-navegar)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add comprehensive logging for connection info debugging

Added detailed logging to help diagnose connection info display issues:

1. Connection Directory Reading:
   - Log connections directory path (V5)
   - Log number of entries found in directory (V5)
   - Log when directory doesn't exist yet (V5)
   - Log each worker's active connections with details (V4)
   - Log total connections across all workers (V4)
   - Log when no connections are found (V5)

2. Connection Reporting:
   - Log when connections are reported to server (V4)
   - Added connection count to worker summary log
   - Changed summary format: restarts -> conns in output
   - Log in both hypervisor and config fallback modes

3. Worker Environment Setup:
   - Log TF_CONNECTION_INFO_PATH for each worker (V4)
   - Helps verify correct file path is set for tensor-fusion-worker

These logs will help diagnose:
- Whether connection files are being created by tensor-fusion-worker
- Whether connection files are being read correctly by agent
- Whether connections are being reported to the server
- Path configuration issues

Note: Connection files are populated by tensor-fusion-worker when
clients connect. If no connections appear, the issue is likely in
tensor-fusion-worker not writing to TF_CONNECTION_INFO_PATH.
